_comment: WC2026 Round of 32, 2026-07-02, San Francisco Bay Area Stadium. KNOCKOUT: no draw -- level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties, so the deliverable is who ADVANCES. Winner meets the Belgium (who beat Senegal) in the Round of 16. Routes: USA WON Group D (4-1 Paraguay, 2-0 Australia, 2-3 Turkey; 6 pts, GF 8 GA 4, rating 79) -- the HOST nation, playing AT HOME in the Bay Area; ATTACKING (8 goals) but with a LEAKY back line (lost 2-3 to Turkey, conceded 3 in that game, GA 4). Bosnia & Herz. qualified as a BEST THIRD from Group B (1-1 Canada, 1-4 Switzerland, 3-1 Qatar; 4 pts, GF 5 GA 6, GD -1, rating 72) -- can score (5 goals, blew past Qatar 3-1, held Canada) but VERY leaky (shipped 4 to Switzerland, GA 6). Class gap ~7 (79 vs 72) PLUS a real HOST home advantage for USA -> USA the clear favourite. But BOTH sides attack AND concede -> an open, higher-scoring, BTTS-live game (a favourite+goals lean), NOT a low-block grind. Bosnia is a dangerous, leaky underdog that can nick one; lower shootout risk than the round's tight defensive ties, but not zero. Home = USA (REAL home, Bay Area). Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.venue: World Cup 2026 - San Francisco Bay Area (USA HOME)
meta.stakes: Single-elimination: level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties. Winner faces Belgium in the R16. USA a clear favourite (won Group D, rating 79, HOST at home in the Bay Area) vs an attacking-but-very-leaky Bosnia (best third, rating 72). Both sides score and concede -> an open, higher-scoring, BTTS-live tie (favourite+goals), lower shootout risk than the tight ties but a live Bosnia.

home.name: USA
notes: Host, won Group D and ATTACKS (8 goals: 4-1 Paraguay, 2-0 Australia). vs a leaky Bosnia -> should get plenty of chances. attack folded into base.
LEAKY at the back (lost 2-3 to Turkey, conceded 3; GA 4). Not a clean-sheet machine -> Bosnia can score. defense neutral.
Clear favourite by class + HOST home advantage in the Bay Area (baked into base_home 1.35).

away.name: Bosnia & Herz.
notes: Can score (5 goals, 3-1 Qatar, held Canada 1-1) -> a real threat vs a leaky USA, not a low block. attack folded into base_away.
VERY leaky (shipped 4 to Switzerland, GA 6, GD -1) -> USA should break through. defense neutral.
Dangerous underdog that can nick one; open, end-to-end game, some ET/penalty risk but lower than the tight ties.

_recal: First pass landed at USA 55.4 / Draw 23.9 / Bosnia 20.6 vs market de-vig 55.6 / 25.5 / 18.9 -- max divergence 1.7 pts, ALIGNED on the first attempt, no lift/trim needed. USA a clear host favourite in an open, higher-scoring tie.

_postmatch.result: USA 0-2 Bosnia & Herz. The host is ELIMINATED at home; Bosnia advance to the Round of 16 (where they meet Belgium). Settled in 90', a clean sheet for the underdog -- an outright upset, no shootout.
_postmatch.model_1x2: USA 55.4 / Draw 23.9 / Bosnia 20.6 (MEDIUM, 90'); advancement USA 69.6 / Bosnia 30.4
_postmatch.verdict: A priced-tail upset, not a process error -- but a clear headline MISS. The model (and the market, 55.6%) made USA the clear favourite to advance (69.6%); Bosnia won outright from the ~20.6% away-win / 30.4% advance branch, and did it in regulation. Two things landed in the low-probability tail at once: (1) USA, the attacking host, was SHUT OUT at home -- the model gave USA only a 15.7% chance of being clean-sheeted (Bosnia CS), and it happened; (2) the exact 0-2 for the away side wasn't in the top-6 scorelines (all USA-leaning). What the pre-match note DID get right: I explicitly flagged USA as ATTACKING-but-LEAKY (lost 2-3 to Turkey, GA 4) and Bosnia as a dangerous, scoring underdog -- the leaky half bit hard (conceded 2) while the attacking half no-showed. This is the downside variance of favouring a leaky side on attack: when the goals dry up, the fragile defence turns a 'favourite+goals' game into a shut-out loss. The market agreed with the lean, so it is genuine variance, not a mispricing.
_postmatch.lesson: A leaky favourite carries real two-way variance: the same open-game read that makes them 'favourite+goals' also means a quiet attacking night leaves their fragile defence exposed to an outright loss -- exactly what happened (USA blanked, Bosnia's 16% clean sheet hit). Unlike the tight ties that grind to a coin-flip (Germany/Netherlands lost on penalties -> 🟡), this favourite was beaten in regulation -> a full miss (❌). Don't retrofit the model: USA 69.6% to advance was honest and market-aligned; roughly one in three of these ties goes to the underdog, and this was one. The host-advantage lift did not save a side that couldn't score on the night.
